expertise_area: Free Solo Climbing, Risk Management, Perseverance

key_insights.insight: It is better to die at 55 doing what you love than to die at 78 regretting not having lived.
key_insights.quote: You're better off dying at 55 of an accident, but having done many things that you're proud of and, you know, like led a life that you're proud of, then dying at 78 and still wishing you had more, but having done none of the things that you wanted to do.

key_insights.insight: People need to stop making life plans, because it limits the serendipity and opportunity.
key_insights.quote: People need to stop making, young people in particular, need to stop making life plans.Because it narrows them to the serendipity and opportunity and things that might happen if they're broad and open minded.

key_insights.insight: Focus on doing what you love and are challenged by, and don't worry about the money or big picture goals.
key_insights.quote: If you just focus on doing lots of little things all the time, occasionally the big things just happen. And you can't totally know ahead of time.

key_insights.insight: There's no trick or 'hack' to overcome fear; it's about getting really scared repeatedly until it's not so scary anymore.
key_insights.quote: There's no hack. You just get really freaking scared over and over for so long. And eventually it's not that scary anymore.

key_insights.insight: If your goal is too big, you need to just get out and do something.
key_insights.quote: Like you need to just get out and like do a little thing.You need to achieve some success.You need to see that you can do somethingand you need to take on appropriately sized challenges

key_insights.insight: You are going to die no matter what, you might as well take smart calculated risks and do all the things that you want.
key_insights.quote: You can take no risk in your life and you're still going freaking die. So you might as well take smart calculated risks and do all the things that you want to doand at least die happy when you go.

key_insights.insight: Intentionality is key when evaluating risk.
key_insights.quote: At least I'm taking the risks that I'm choosing...I'm choosing to take the risks and I'm pretty clear-eyed about the risk that I'm taking.

key_insights.insight: When breaking down complex challenges, it's important to study and make notes to learn something the way you study anything else.
key_insights.quote: I had a note on my phone just like writing down all the different, basically I'm trying to learn it the way you study anything where I'm just like making notes.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice exposure therapy to conquer your fears.
actionable_advice.how_to: Repeatedly expose yourself to the source of your fear in a controlled environment, gradually increasing the intensity.

actionable_advice.advice: Break down large, daunting goals into smaller, manageable steps.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify the individual components of the overall goal and focus on mastering each one before moving on to the next.

actionable_advice.advice: Choose actions that you want to take, make choices, and use your time in a way you want to use it.
actionable_advice.how_to: Make sure to ask yourself if something is aligned with your values. Intentionally pursue actions you have been wanting to take but have been putting off. Make plans now and execute them.

memorable_stories: Alex Honnold lived in a van for 10 years to save money and pursue climbing.
Alex Honnold climbed Taipei 101 on live television.
Alex describes moments where he was in Antarctica and had a huge amount of fear. The rest days were just spent traumatized.
Story about being trapped at the top of Half Dome.
contrarian_claims: Climbing is safer than people think, and is as or more safe than sports like skiing.
Focus on having things you find valuable, and living in line with your values rather than making life plans.
People need to be taking more calculated risks. People also don't think enough about the risks they aren't choosing to take.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Greatest Climber Alive: I Shouldn't Have Attempted That Climb!
